qa_pairs.question: Where can I find further questions about the Resource Allocation Competitions (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: You can visit the Resource Allocation Competitions (RAC) page for further questions.

qa_pairs.question: What should an Academic Principal Investigator (PI) do if they have delegated their RAC application to a sponsored user?
qa_pairs.answer: If an Academic PI has delegated their application to a sponsored user, the delegated person should follow the outlined steps to submit on their behalf.

qa_pairs.question: What is the first step for an Academic PI to submit a CCV for a RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: The first step is to navigate to the Resource Application page on CCDB at https://ccdb.computecanada.ca/allocation/resource_applications.

qa_pairs.question: Where in the application form should an Academic PI enter their CCV confirmation number?
qa_pairs.answer: In the application form, scroll down to the 'Canadian Common CV' section to enter the submission confirmation number.

qa_pairs.question: What type of CCV confirmation number is accepted for an Alliance RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: Only confirmation numbers for the Alliance template will be accepted for an Alliance RAC application.

qa_pairs.question: What happens after an Academic PI clicks the 'Submit CCV' button if the submission is successful?
qa_pairs.answer: If the submission is successfully processed, the PI's publications will be uploaded and shown in the CCV Publications Reporting section.

qa_pairs.question: Who should an Academic PI contact if they receive an error on CCDB but are sure their CCV submission was successful?
qa_pairs.answer: If an Academic PI receives an error on CCDB despite being sure of a successful CCV submission, they should contact support@tech.alliancecancomputecanada.ca for assistance.

qa_pairs.question: What is required of all Co-Principal Investigators (Co-PIs) for a RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: All Co-PIs of the RAC application must have an updated CCV on file, which means the CCV must have been submitted within the last 90 days.

qa_pairs.question: What is the deadline for Co-PIs to submit their CCV for a RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: Co-PIs must have their CCVs submitted within the last 90 days for a RAC application.

qa_pairs.question: Can an Academic PI submit their RAC application if all Co-PIs have not met the CCV requirement?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the Academic PI will not be able to submit their RAC application until all Co-PIs' CCVs meet the 90-day submission requirement.

qa_pairs.question: Where can an Academic PI view the status of CCVs for Co-PIs in a RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: In the RAC application form, the PI will be able to see the status of the CCV for each Co-PI included in the application, as well as any required action.

qa_pairs.question: What are the two ways a Co-PI can update their CCV?
qa_pairs.answer: Co-PIs can update their CCV either through the Resource Applications page or the Reporting page.

qa_pairs.question: How can a Co-PI update their CCV from the Resource Applications page?
qa_pairs.answer: From the Resource Applications page, Co-PIs can click on 'Resource Applications' in the menu, navigate to the application where they are listed 'As a Co-PI of' under the 'Ongoing Competitions' tab, and then click on 'Update CCV'.

qa_pairs.question: How can a Co-PI update their CCV using the Reporting page?
qa_pairs.answer: A Co-PI can update their CCV by clicking on 'View reporting' under the 'My Account' menu.

qa_pairs.question: Why is it important to report publications enabled by computational resources?
qa_pairs.answer: Reporting more publications helps build a stronger case for additional compute and storage resources, and the CCV is also an input for the scientific review process of RAC applications.

qa_pairs.question: What happens in the 'CCV Publications Reporting' section when an Academic PI submits a new or updated CCV?
qa_pairs.answer: The publications included in the Alliance CCV template will show in the 'CCV Publications Reporting' section of the CCV submission form on CCDB, surrounded by a red square.

qa_pairs.question: What does a red border around a publication in the 'CCV Publications Reporting' section indicate?
qa_pairs.answer: A red border indicates that an answer has not yet been provided for that publication regarding whether it was enabled by computational resources.

qa_pairs.question: What must an Academic PI do with all publications to submit their RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: An Academic PI must provide an answer for all publications, ensuring there are no red borders left, in order to submit their RAC application.

qa_pairs.question: How can an Academic PI quickly mark all new publications as enabled by computational resources?
qa_pairs.answer: An Academic PI can click 'Select all new' to mark all new publications as enabled by the resources.

qa_pairs.question: What button should an Academic PI click if none of the new publications uploaded were enabled by computational resources?
qa_pairs.answer: An Academic PI should click on the 'Clear all new' button if none of the new publications uploaded were enabled by computational resources.

qa_pairs.question: How many times must an Academic PI click on a publication to mark it as *not* enabled by computational resources when going one-by-one?
qa_pairs.answer: When going one-by-one, an Academic PI has to click twice on a publication to mark it as not enabled by our resources; the first click indicates it was enabled, and the second click removes the check mark to indicate it was not.
